schedule.spot_round: Late July 2025 (if seats remain after CAP rounds)

cutoff.note: Cutoffs vary by branch and change every year based on demand. Computer Engineering has the highest cutoff.

eligibility.percentage: 50% aggregate in PCM (45% for reserved categories)
eligibility.entrance_exam: MHT-CET or JEE Main score required
eligibility.age_limit: No upper age limit for engineering admissions in Maharashtra
eligibility.lateral_entry: Diploma holders can apply for direct second year admission via MHT-CET (diploma) exam

steps: Appear for MHT-CET or JEE Main entrance exam
Register on DTE Maharashtra CAP portal at cetcell.mahacet.org
Fill CAP application form and upload documents online
Participate in CAP rounds (online preference filling)
Receive allotment letter after each CAP round
Report to college for document verification
Pay tuition fees to confirm admission
process.cap_rounds: Typically 3 CAP rounds are conducted. Students can upgrade in subsequent rounds.
process.note: Management quota and NRI quota admissions are handled directly by the college.

quotas.management_quota: 15% seats — direct admission by college management
quotas.lateral_entry: 10% additional seats for diploma holders in second year

reservation.note: Reservation norms as per Maharashtra government rules

fees.scholarship_note: SC/ST/OBC/EBC students may be eligible for government scholarships via MahaDBT portal.

submission.office: Student Section, Ground Floor, Admin Block
submission.timing: 9 AM to 4 PM on working days
